mnemonics.title: DUC Connection Steps
mnemonics.content: Driver, URL, Connect - Remember the 3-step process: Load Driver (Class.forName), Create URL (jdbc:odbc:...), Get Connection (DriverManager)

mnemonics.title: Type 2 vs Type 4
mnemonics.content: Type 2 = Two Layers (JDBC -> ODBC -> DB), Type 4 = Four Letters (Pure Java - J-D-B-C)

mnemonics.title: Bridge ISP
mnemonics.content: Key Features: Independence (DB & Platform), Security, Platform Support

mnemonics.title: CLASS Key
mnemonics.content: Class.forName() is crucial - 'Cannot Load And Start Successfully' without loading driver class

acronyms.usage: Helps recall the bridge's role as a Java-to-DB job connector

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a 3-layer bridge: Java App (top) -> JDBC-ODBC Bridge (middle) -> ODBC Data Source (bottom)
Picture a Java class loader inserting a USB driver into JVM
Visualize jdbc:odbc:MyDB as a map: Java Country -> ODBC Bridge -> Database City
See it as a translator book: Java (English) <-> ODBC (French) <-> DB (German)

analogies.analogy: Like an interpreter at a UN meeting - translates Java's requests to ODBC's language
Similar to saving a WiFi network - once configured, devices can connect using saved credentials
Acts like a taxi dispatcher - matches connection requests with available drivers
Like making a phone call: Dial number (URL) -> Verify SIM (driver) -> Connect (call established)
